Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

First thanks for doing this. I was just looking at this work but you beat me to it. I do suggest you move this to libcfs/include/libcfs/linux/linux-wait.h since this will be used by future LNet work as well. Also I did open a ticket for this work - LU-10467. 
Please use that LU number since this work will be lost in the LU-6142 noise.

Patch Set 3:

Will do.   Is there some way I can subscribe to get notifications of all new LU tickets?  I've done that for gerrit, but cannot find any thing similar for jira.

Patch Set 5:

So I tried this patch and it breaks mounting. I believe the solution is to use real autoconf test to detect the presence of this newer functionality.

Patch Set 5:

Thanks for testing.
Did you just test this one patch?  What kernel did you build with?

Do you have a specific reason to think that autoconf would do a better job?  It is entirely possible there is still a bug in the code.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Neil - James has essentially said this, but there's an issue with this series of patches that is causing, what looks like, mounting MDT issues. I think the issue starts with this patch. 

If you look at the logs at https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/86a9e4bc-ce2e-11e9-a25b-52540065bddc , you'll see an error with the MDS (vm9) on the console log:
[  127.086615] LDISKFS-fs (dm-6): mounted filesystem with ordered data mode. Opts: user_xattr,errors=remount-ro,no_mbcache,nodelalloc
[  127.101554] 6Lustre: osd-ldiskfs create tunables for lustre-MDT0000
[  188.204132] 3LustreError: 15f-b: lustre-MDT0000: cannot register this server with the MGS: rc = -110. Is the MGS running?

Patch Set 5:

Thanks -- I had seen those messages, and James comments, but I hadn't managed to put it all together properly.  I think I have now.
The problem is (I think) my change to add_wait_queue_exclusive_head() to add the test on list_empty().
This is sometimes needed, but sometimes the list_head isn't initialized so it does the wrong thing.  I'll re-think that.

Thanks.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 6:

PS: I think this patch could be refreshed with the fixed comments without having to resubmit the whole series, since it is the first patch in the series.  Something like:

 $ git rebase -i HEAD~18                        # get to this patch
 $ vi libcfs/include/libcfs/linux/linux-wait.h  # fix this patch
 $ git commit -a --amend
 $ git push ...                                 # push only this patch

Patch Set 6:

Never mind my previous comment about not rebasing the whole series.  I forgot that we just landed a patch to revert the ZFS support from 0.8.1 to 0.7.13, since we are getting a lot of test failures in the ZFS tests, so you may as well rebase the whole series to avoid the high rate of spurious test failures.

parents.subject: LU-12681 osc: wrong cache of LVB attrs, part2

commit.subject: LU-10467 lustre: add wait_event macros suitable for upstream
commit.message:
LU-10467 lustre: add wait_event macros suitable for upstream

This patch adds three sorts of wait_event macros.

1/ wait_event_idle_* which are available upstream, but not
   in older kernels.
   if TASK_NOLOAD is not available, we use TASK_UNINTERRUPTIBLE,
   and block all interrupts.

   We cannot use ___wait_cond_timeout() as it changed signature
   in 3.13. so we define our own ___wait_cond_timeout1().

2/ wait_event_idle_exclusive_lifo() and
   wait_event_idle_exclusive_lifo_timeout()
    which might be accepted upstream if we can make a strong case

   prepare_to_wait_event() doesn't support this directly, but
   as it won't relink a wait_entry that is already linked, it
   is sufficient to link to the head of the queue before calling
   prepare_to_wait_event().

3/ l_wait_event_abortable
   l_wait_event_abortable_timeout
   l_wait_event_abortable_exclusive
    which are unlikely to be accepted upstream, but match the general
    approach of upstream wait_event macros, and are useful
    to lustre.
    Possibly some or all of these should become wait_event_killable_*
    LUSTRE_FATAL_SIGS is moved over to linux-wait.h.

___wait_event() and related macros are copied from upstream linux,
and modified slightly to work across all supported kernels.
